_meta.register: Roofing owner-operator B2B — plain English peer respect. No agency jargon. No emojis. No high-pressure.
_meta.framework: Jeremy Miner NEPQ neutral-reverse + PAS soft-pivot. Acknowledge → reframe → leave them in control.

NOT_INTERESTED.label: Not interested / busy / hang up
comebacks: Totally fair — most calls like this don't earn the time. Two seconds though: I'm not asking for a meeting, I want to send you a free Roofing Site Conversion Audit on {company}. Email it, done. Worst case you read it in 5 minutes and trash it.
I get it. Real quick before you go — if I sent you a 1-page summary of the 3 specific things on {company}'s homepage that are quietly costing you bookings, would you read it? No call needed.
Understood. Last thing — one fix I'd send you in the audit is probably worth a job or two. If it doesn't read like that, ignore the rest. Best email?

REBUILT.label: We just rebuilt the site / new site
comebacks: Good — that means you've already invested. The audit is even more useful then, because I can tell you what your new site is still missing for conversion vs how it looks. Built sites usually look great but leak on the same 3 spots. Want me to send it?
Makes sense. Most rebuilds focus on look and not on what the homeowner does in the first 3 seconds. The audit covers that gap. Free, 48 hours, no pitch on the call.
Great — so you know the site cost real money. I can show you whether that money's converting. That's the whole audit.

EMAIL_INSTEAD.label: Send me an email / put it in writing
comebacks: Yeah that's the plan — I'll email the audit. Two questions: best email for you, and is there 1 thing on {company} you want me to look at first?
Will do. Quick — confirm the best email and I'll have it to you in 48 hours. Reply if anything sparks a question.
Sending right now. What's the best email?

PRICE.label: How much / what's it cost
comebacks: Audit's free — about $3,000 of work but you get it because I do 5 a week and roughly 1 in 5 turn into clients. That's enough math for me. No pitch on the call.
Free. I make my money on the ones who hire me after, not on the audit. About 1 in 5 do.
Audit itself costs you nothing. If you decide to fix things and want help, separate conversation — we can talk numbers then.

PARTNER.label: Need to talk to my partner / wife / GM
comebacks: Totally — most owners do. The audit's actually the perfect thing to share with them. I'll send the PDF, you read it together, no decision needed before then.
Smart move. The audit's a one-pager summary on top of the deep PDF — easy to show your partner. What's the best email?
Of course — that's why I'm sending a written audit. They can read it on their own time. Best email?

BROKERS.label: We don't deal with lead-gen / brokers / agencies
comebacks: Not what I do. I don't sell leads. I do a written conversion audit on your homepage so you can fix the leaks yourself or hand it to whoever you want. No leads, no commission, no ongoing fees.
Hear you. This isn't a lead-gen pitch — it's a 1-time written analysis of your homepage. Same way you'd inspect a roof before bidding it. Free.
Different thing. Lead-gen sells you a phone number. I'm sending you a teardown of what's wrong with your site. Then it's your call.

ADS_WORK.label: Our ads work / we get plenty of business
comebacks: Good problem to have. The audit still helps — because it shows you how much MORE you'd be getting if the homepage didn't leak the way it does. Even busy contractors usually find a 10-20% lift hiding in 2 or 3 fixes.
Glad to hear it. Audit's free — worst case you read it, see your conversion's at industry-best already, and feel good about it. About 9 out of 10 contractors find at least one $5K-$10K leak.
That's great. Ask yourself though — if your homepage is leaking 20% of qualified clicks, you're paying for that ghost-traffic every month. The audit puts a number on it. Free, no pitch.

AGENCY.label: We already have an agency / marketing company
comebacks: Cool — agencies usually run the ads and the SEO, but very few do a real conversion audit on the homepage itself. That's the gap I fill. You can share the audit with your agency — most appreciate a fresh outside read.
Good. The audit pairs well with what they do — it's the layer between ads and your homepage. Most agencies don't pay for that piece. Send it to your team after you read it.
Makes sense. This isn't to replace them. It's a free written second-opinion on what your homepage is doing with the traffic they're sending. Worth 5 minutes.
